title: Architecture detection uses platform.processor() (often empty on Linux) instead of platform.machine()
summary: get_system_arch() in src/parca_agent.py:26 reads the host architecture with platform.processor(). On Linux platform.processor() returns the same value as 'uname -p', which is frequently an empty string (or 'unknown') because most distros don't populate it. When it returns '', get_system_arch() returns '' (the if/elif don't match), the module-level ARCH becomes '', target_revision (which looks up _snap_revisions[(confinement, ARCH)]) returns None, and ParcaAgent.install() then raises SnapSpecError ('parca-agent snap is not supported for arch=...') on an otherwise-supported amd64/arm64 host. The function's own docstring describes machine-style values (x86_64, aarch64, arm64, armv8b, armv8l), which are exactly what platform.machine() returns reliably.
why_good_first_issue: One-line fix (swap platform.processor() for platform.machine()) plus one mock-based unit test. No architecture or Juju knowledge needed; fully reproducible locally without a deploy. The correct approach is already the de-facto standard in the sibling Canonical Observability code (cos-lib's get_host_architecture and craft tooling use platform.machine()). Clear 'done': arch detection no longer collapses to '' when processor() is empty, and a test asserts it.
affected_files: src/parca_agent.py; tests/unit/test_charm/conftest.py; tests/unit/ (new test for get_system_arch)
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/parca-agent-operator ~/work/parca-agent-operator. Baseline: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/parca-agent-operator && export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH && tox -e unit'. Then run a tiny repro that mocks platform.processor to '' and reloads src.parca_agent: with unittest.mock.patch('platform.processor', lambda: ''): importlib.reload(parca_agent); print(parca_agent.get_system_arch(), parca_agent.ARCH); ParcaAgent('parca-agent', {'remote-store-address':'x'}, set()).target_revision. Run via: PYTHONPATH=.:lib:src uv run --frozen --isolated --extra=dev python repro.py.
repro_result: confirmed: baseline tox -e unit = 22 passed, 76% coverage in ~6s. On the cos VM platform.processor()=='x86_64' and platform.machine()=='x86_64', but when platform.processor() is patched to '' (its documented common Linux value) get_system_arch() returns '' and module ARCH=='', so ParcaAgent(...).target_revision is None -> install() raises SnapSpecError ('not supported for arch='). platform.machine() returns 'x86_64' on the same host, so switching to machine() fixes it. Observed-vs-expected: expected 'amd64', got '' (and an install failure) whenever processor() is empty.
fix_hint: In src/parca_agent.py change 'arch = platform.processor()' (line 26) to 'arch = platform.machine()'. Add a unit test that patches platform.machine to return 'aarch64' and asserts get_system_arch() == 'arm64', and one that patches it to 'x86_64' and asserts 'amd64'. Optionally reuse cos-lib's get_host_architecture helper if/when it is published, to avoid duplicating the alias map.
notes: No existing issue/PR references arch/processor/machine detection (gh issue & pr search returned empty). The two OPEN issues in this repo (#110 and #41) are both the identical automated 'chore: update libraries to new major versions' (update charms.operator_libs_linux.v1.snap to v2); a major-version lib bump can carry breaking API changes and is not a clean beginner task, so both picks here are self-identified.

title: parse_version() docstring references 'parca --version' but the command run is 'parca-agent --version'
summary: parse_version() in src/parca_agent.py:196 has the docstring "Parse the output of 'parca --version' and return a representative string." but this is the parca-AGENT operator, and the only caller (ParcaAgent.version, src/parca_agent.py:179) runs check_output(['parca-agent', '--version']). The docstring names the wrong binary ('parca' instead of 'parca-agent'), which is misleading for anyone reading or maintaining the parser.
why_good_first_issue: Pure one-word documentation correction in a single file; no behaviour change, no tests to rewrite. Clear, objective 'done': the docstring names the same command the code actually executes. Verifiable purely by inspection.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/parca-agent-operator && grep -n "parca --version\|parca-agent.*--version\|parse_version" src/parca_agent.py'. Shows the caller at line 179 running ['parca-agent', '--version'] and the docstring at line 196 saying 'parca --version'.
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: src/parca_agent.py:179 calls check_output(['parca-agent', '--version']), but src/parca_agent.py:196 docstring says "Parse the output of 'parca --version' ...". The binary name in the docstring ('parca') does not match the command actually executed ('parca-agent').
fix_hint: Edit the docstring at src/parca_agent.py:196 to read "Parse the output of 'parca-agent --version' and return a representative string."
notes: Found while reading src/parca_agent.py for the arch bug above. Lower-priority cleanup candidate in the same file (not picked, needs a judgement call): the TODO at src/parca_agent.py:73 'is app_name enough to avoid collisions with other charms' certs?'.
